name: German-Style Stuffed Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 (1 pound) pork tenderloin, trimmed of fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a lengthwise slit down the center of the pork tenderloin, 2/3 of the way through the meat.
2. Place the tenderloin between two sheets of wax paper and flatten to 1/4 inch thickness.
3. Combine the tenderloin and next three ingredients (raisins, beer, cider vinegar) in a baking dish. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for 4 hours, turning occasionally.
4. Combine bread crumbs, orange rind, and garlic; cover and let stand for 4 hours.
5. Remove the tenderloin from the marinade and strain the marinade, reserving the raisins. Discard the marinade.
6. Add the reserved raisins to the breadcrumb mixture.
7. Combine brown sugar, salt, pepper, and cinnamon in a small bowl.
8. Rub the seasoning mixture over both sides of the tenderloin.
9. Spread the breadcrumb mixture evenly over the tenderloin, stopping 1/2 inch from the edges.
10. Roll up the tenderloin jelly-roll style, starting at the narrow end.
11. Tie securely with heavy string at 2-inch intervals.
12. Place the rolled pork on a rack in a shallow roasting pan.
13. Bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 55 minutes. Serve four.
